definitions.description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.

builderHint.searchHint: For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model. This OpenAI node is for specialized operations: image generation (DALL-E), audio (Whisper, TTS), and video generation (Sora).

builderHint.propertyHint: For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model instead of this resource.

options.name: Generate Audio
options.description: Creates audio from a text prompt

options.name: Transcribe a Recording
options.description: Transcribes audio into text

options.name: Translate a Recording
options.description: Translates audio into text in English

properties.displayName: OpenAI API limits the size of the audio file to 25 MB
properties.name: fileSizeLimitNotice
input
properties.placeholder: e.g. The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog
properties.description: The text to generate audio for. The maximum length is 4096 characters.

properties.name: voice
properties.description: The voice to use when generating the audio

options.name: binaryPropertyOutput
options.hint: The name of the output field to put the binary file data in

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the audio file in one of these formats: flac, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, ogg, wav, or webm

options.name: language
options.description: The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in ISO-639-1 format will improve accuracy and latency.

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the audio file in one of these formats: flac, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, ogg, wav, or webm

options.name: Delete a File
options.description: Delete a file from the server

options.name: List Files
options.description: Returns a list of files that belong to the user's organization

options.name: Upload a File
options.description: Upload a file that can be used across various endpoints

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the file. The size of individual files can be a maximum of 512 MB or 2 million tokens for Assistants.

options.name: purpose
options.description: The intended purpose of the uploaded file, the 'Fine-tuning' only supports .jsonl files

options.name: purpose
options.description: Only return files with the given purpose

options.name: Analyze Image
options.description: Take in images and answer questions about them

options.name: Generate an Image
options.description: Creates an image from a text prompt

options.name: Edit Image
options.description: Edit an image

properties.name: model
properties.description: The model to use for image generation

properties.name: prompt
properties.placeholder: e.g. A cute cat eating a dinosaur
properties.description: A text description of the desired image(s). The maximum length is 1000 characters for dall-e-2 and 4000 characters for dall-e-3.

options.name: n
options.description: Number of images to generate

options.name: dalleQuality
options.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, HD creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

options.name: quality
options.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, High creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

options.name: Natural
options.description: Produce more natural looking images

options.name: Vivid
options.description: Lean towards generating hyper-real and dramatic images

options.name: returnImageUrls
options.description: Whether to return image URL(s) instead of binary file(s)

options.name: binaryPropertyOutput
options.hint: The name of the output field to put the binary file data in

options.name: n
options.description: Number of images to generate

options.name: dalleQuality
options.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, HD creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

options.name: quality
options.description: The quality of the image that will be generated, High creates images with finer details and greater consistency across the image

options.name: Natural
options.description: Produce more natural looking images

options.name: Vivid
options.description: Lean towards generating hyper-real and dramatic images

options.name: returnImageUrls
options.description: Whether to return image URL(s) instead of binary file(s)

options.name: binaryPropertyOutput
options.hint: The name of the output field to put the binary file data in

properties.name: imageUrls
properties.description: URL(s) of the image(s) to analyze, multiple URLs can be added separated by comma

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image(s)

properties.name: simplify
properties.description: Whether to simplify the response or not

options.name: Auto
options.description: Model will look at the image input size and decide if it should use the low or high setting

options.name: Low
options.description: Return faster responses and consume fewer tokens

options.name: High
options.description: Return more detailed responses, consumes more tokens
Fewer tokens will result in shorter, less detailed image description
options.name: maxTokens

properties.name: model
properties.description: The model to use for image generation

properties.name: prompt
properties.description: A text description of the desired image(s). Maximum 1000 characters for dall-e-2, 32000 characters for gpt-image-1.

properties.name: images
properties.description: Add one or more binary fields to include images with your prompt. Each image should be a png, webp, or jpg file less than 50MB. You can provide up to 16 images.

values.name: binaryPropertyName
values.description: The name of the binary field containing the image data

properties.name: images
properties.description: Add one or more binary fields to include images with your prompt. Each image should be a png, webp, or jpg file less than 50MB. You can provide up to 16 images.

values.name: binaryPropertyName
values.description: The name of the binary field containing the image data

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. It should be a square png file less than 4MB.

properties.name: binaryPropertyName
properties.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
properties.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. It should be a square png file less than 4MB.

properties.name: n
properties.description: The number of images to generate. Must be between 1 and 10.

properties.name: size
properties.description: The size of the generated images

properties.name: quality
properties.description: The quality of the image that will be generated

properties.name: quality
properties.description: The quality of the image that will be generated

properties.name: responseFormat
properties.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. URLs are only valid for 60 minutes after generation.

properties.name: responseFormat
properties.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. URLs are only valid for 60 minutes after generation.

properties.name: outputFormat
properties.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

properties.name: outputFormat
properties.description: The format in which the generated images are returned. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

properties.name: outputCompression
properties.description: The compression level (0-100%) for the generated images. Only supported for gpt-image-1 with webp or jpeg output formats.

properties.name: outputCompression
properties.description: The compression level (0-100%) for the generated images. Only supported for gpt-image-1 with webp or jpeg output formats.

options.name: user
options.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse

options.name: background
options.description: Allows to set transparency for the background of the generated image(s). Only supported for gpt-image-1.

options.name: inputFidelity
options.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features of input images. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

options.name: imageMask
options.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
options.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. An additional image whose fully transparent areas (e.g. where alpha is zero) indicate where image should be edited. If there are multiple images provided, the mask will be applied on the first image. Must be a valid PNG file, less than 4MB, and have the same dimensions as image.

options.name: user
options.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse

options.name: background
options.description: Allows to set transparency for the background of the generated image(s). Only supported for gpt-image-1.

options.name: inputFidelity
options.description: Control how much effort the model will exert to match the style and features of input images. Only supported for gpt-image-1.

options.name: imageMask
options.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
options.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image. An additional image whose fully transparent areas (e.g. where alpha is zero) indicate where image should be edited. If there are multiple images provided, the mask will be applied on the first image. Must be a valid PNG file, less than 4MB, and have the same dimensions as image.

options.name: Message a Model
options.description: Generate a model response with GPT 3, 4, 5, etc. using Responses API

builderHint.propertyHint: Use operation: 'response' for text generation. Do NOT use 'message' — that v1 value is invalid on v2 and fails validation.

options.name: Classify Text for Violations
options.description: Check whether content complies with usage policies

properties.name: input
properties.description: The input text to classify if it is violates the moderation policy

properties.name: simplify
properties.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

options.name: useStableModel
options.description: Whether to use the stable version of the model instead of the latest version, accuracy may be slightly lower

values.name: role
values.description: Role in shaping the model's response, it tells the model how it should behave and interact with the user

options.name: User
options.description: Send a message as a user and get a response from the model

options.name: Assistant
options.description: Tell the model to adopt a specific tone or personality

options.name: System
options.description: Usually used to set the model's behavior or context for the next user message

values.name: content
values.description: The content of the message to be send
values.placeholder: e.g. Hello, how can you help me?

values.name: imageUrl
values.description: URL of the image to be sent

values.name: binaryPropertyName
values.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
values.description: Name of the binary property which contains the image(s)

values.name: fileId
values.description: ID of the file to be sent

values.name: imageDetail
values.description: The detail level of the image to be sent to the model

values.name: fileUrl
values.description: URL of the file to be sent. Accepts base64 encoded files as well.

values.name: fileId
values.description: ID of the file to be sent

values.name: binaryPropertyName
values.hint: The name of the input field containing the binary file data to be processed
values.description: Name of the binary property which contains the file

properties.name: simplify
properties.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

properties.displayName: Connect your own custom n8n tools to this node on the canvas
properties.name: noticeTools

options.description: High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the search
options.name: searchContextSize
allowedDomains
options.description: Comma-separated list of domains to search. Only domains in this list will be searched.

options.name: vectorStoreIds
options.description: The vector store IDs to use for the file search. Vector stores are managed via OpenAI Dashboard.

options.name: codeInterpreter
options.description: Whether to allow the model to execute code in a sandboxed environment

options.name: conversationId
options.description: The conversation that this response belongs to. Input items and output items from this response are automatically added to this conversation after this response completes.

options.name: include
options.description: Specify additional output data to include in the model response

options.name: instructions
options.description: Instructions for the model to follow

options.name: maxTokens
options.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. Most models have a context length of 2048 tokens (except for the newest models, which support 32,768).

options.name: maxToolsIterations
options.description: The maximum number of tool iteration cycles the LLM will run before stopping. A single iteration can contain multiple tool calls. Set to 0 for no limit.

options.name: maxToolCalls
options.description: The maximum number of total calls to built-in tools that can be processed in a response. This maximum number applies across all built-in tool calls, not per individual tool. Any further attempts to call a tool by the model will be ignored.

options.name: metadata
options.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard. Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings with a maximum length of 512 characters.

options.name: parallelToolCalls
options.description: Whether to allow parallel tool calls. If true, the model can call multiple tools at once.

options.name: previousResponseId
options.description: The ID of the previous response to continue from. Cannot be used in conjunction with Conversation ID.

options.name: promptConfig
options.description: Configure the reusable prompt template configured via OpenAI Dashboard. Learn more.

values.name: promptId
values.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use

values.name: version
values.description: Optional version of the prompt template

values.name: variables
values.description: Variables to be substituted into the prompt template

options.name: promptCacheKey
options.description: Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates

values.name: summary
values.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.

options.name: safetyIdentifier
options.description: A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies. The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user.

options.name: serviceTier
options.description: The service tier to use for the request

options.name: store
options.description: Whether to store the generated model response for later retrieval via API

values.name: name
values.description: The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

values.displayName: All properties in the schema must be set to "required", when using "strict" mode.
values.name: requiredNotice
schema
values.description: The schema of the response format

values.name: description
values.description: The description of the response format

values.name: strict
values.description: Whether to require that the AI will always generate responses that match the provided JSON Schema

options.name: topLogprobs
options.description: An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability

options.name: temperature
options.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both

options.name: topP
options.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: truncation
options.description: Whether to truncate the input to the model's context window size. When disabled will throw a 400 error instead.

values.name: enabled
values.description: Whether to run the model in background mode. If true, the model will run in background mode.

values.name: timeout
values.description: The timeout for the background mode in seconds. If 0, the timeout is infinite.

options.name: Generate
options.description: Creates a video from a text prompt

properties.name: prompt
properties.default: A video of a cat playing with a ball
properties.description: The prompt to generate a video from

properties.name: seconds
properties.description: Clip duration in seconds

properties.name: size
properties.description: Output resolution formatted as width x height. 1024x1792 and 1792x1024 are only supported by Sora 2 Pro.

options.description: Optional image reference that guides generation
options.name: binaryPropertyNameReference
waitTime
options.description: Time to wait for the video to be generated in seconds

options.name: fileName
options.hint: The name of the output field to put the binary file data in

definitions.name: @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi

definitions.description: Message an assistant or GPT, analyze images, generate audio, etc.

builderHint.searchHint: For text generation, reasoning and tools, use AI Agent with OpenAI Chat Model. This OpenAI node is for specialized operations: image generation (DALL-E), audio (Whisper, TTS), and video generation (Sora).

options.name: Create an Assistant
options.description: Create a new assistant

options.name: Delete an Assistant
options.description: Delete an assistant from the account

options.name: List Assistants
options.description: List assistants in the organization

options.name: Message an Assistant
options.description: Send messages to an assistant

options.name: Update an Assistant
options.description: Update an existing assistant

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the assistant. The maximum length is 256 characters.

properties.name: description
properties.description: The description of the assistant. The maximum length is 512 characters.

properties.name: instructions
properties.description: The system instructions that the assistant uses. The maximum length is 32768 characters.

properties.name: codeInterpreter
properties.description: Whether to enable the code interpreter that allows the assistants to write and run Python code in a sandboxed execution environment, find more here

properties.name: knowledgeRetrieval
properties.description: Whether to augments the assistant with knowledge from outside its model, such as proprietary product information or documents, find more here

properties.name: file_ids
properties.description: The files to be used by the assistant, there can be a maximum of 20 files attached to the assistant. You can use expression to pass file IDs as an array or comma-separated string.
properties.hint: Add more files by using the 'Upload a File' operation

properties.name: file_ids
properties.description: The files to be used by the assistant, there can be a maximum of 20 files attached to the assistant
properties.hint: Add more files by using the 'Upload a File' operation

properties.name: file_ids
properties.description: The files to be used by the assistant, there can be a maximum of 20 files attached to the assistant
properties.hint: Add more files by using the 'Upload a File' operation

properties.displayName: Add custom n8n tools when you message your assistant (rather than when creating it)
properties.name: noticeTools

options.name: temperature
options.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: topP
options.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: failIfExists
options.description: Whether to fail an operation if the assistant with the same name already exists

properties.name: assistantId
properties.description: Assistant to respond to the message. You can add, modify or remove assistants in the playground.

properties.name: assistantId
properties.description: Assistant to respond to the message. You can add, modify or remove assistants in the playground.

options.name: Connected Chat Trigger Node
options.description: Looks for an input field called 'chatInput' that is coming from a directly connected Chat Trigger

options.name: Connected Guardrails Node
options.description: Looks for an input field called 'guardrailsInput' that is coming from a directly connected Guardrails Node

options.name: Define below
options.description: Use an expression to reference data in previous nodes or enter static text

properties.name: text
properties.placeholder: e.g. Hello, how can you help me?

options.name: Use memory connector
options.description: Connect one of the supported memory nodes

options.name: Use thread ID
options.description: Specify the ID of the thread to continue

properties.name: threadId
properties.description: The ID of the thread to continue, a new thread will be created if not specified
properties.hint: If the thread ID is empty or undefined a new thread will be created and included in the response

properties.displayName: Connect your own custom n8n tools to this node on the canvas
properties.name: noticeTools
options
properties.description: Additional options to add

options.name: baseURL
options.description: Override the default base URL for the API

options.name: maxRetries
options.description: Maximum number of retries to attempt

options.name: timeout
options.description: Maximum amount of time a request is allowed to take in milliseconds

options.name: preserveOriginalTools
options.description: Whether to preserve the original tools of the assistant after the execution of this node, otherwise the tools will be replaced with the connected tools, if any, default is true

properties.name: simplify
properties.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

properties.name: assistantId
properties.description: Assistant to respond to the message. You can add, modify or remove assistants in the playground.

options.name: codeInterpreter
options.description: Whether to enable the code interpreter that allows the assistants to write and run Python code in a sandboxed execution environment, find more here

options.name: description
options.description: The description of the assistant. The maximum length is 512 characters.

options.name: file_ids
options.description: The files to be used by the assistant, there can be a maximum of 20 files attached to the assistant. You can use expression to pass file IDs as an array or comma-separated string.
options.hint: Add more files by using the 'Upload a File' operation, any existing files not selected here will be removed.

options.name: instructions
options.description: The system instructions that the assistant uses. The maximum length is 32768 characters.

options.name: knowledgeRetrieval
options.description: Whether to augments the assistant with knowledge from outside its model, such as proprietary product information or documents, find more here

options.name: name
options.description: The name of the assistant. The maximum length is 256 characters.

options.name: removeCustomTools
options.description: Whether to remove all custom tools (functions) from the assistant

options.name: temperature
options.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: topP
options.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

properties.name: simplify
properties.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

properties.name: jsonOutput
properties.description: Whether to attempt to return the response in JSON format. Compatible with GPT-4 Turbo and all GPT-3.5 Turbo models newer than gpt-3.5-turbo-1106.

properties.displayName: Connect your own custom n8n tools to this node on the canvas
properties.name: noticeTools

options.name: frequency_penalty
options.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim

options.name: maxTokens
options.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. Most models have a context length of 2048 tokens (except for the newest models, which support 32,768).

options.name: n
options.description: How many completions to generate for each prompt. Note: Because this parameter generates many completions, it can quickly consume your token quota. Use carefully and ensure that you have reasonable settings for max_tokens and stop.

options.name: presence_penalty
options.description: Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics

options.name: temperature
options.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: topP
options.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, controls diversity via nucleus sampling: 0.5 means half of all likelihood-weighted options are considered. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

options.name: reasoning_effort
options.description: Controls the amount of reasoning tokens to use. A value of "low" will favor speed and economical token usage, "high" will favor more complete reasoning at the cost of more tokens generated and slower responses.

options.name: Low
options.description: Favors speed and economical token usage

options.name: Medium
options.description: Balance between speed and reasoning accuracy

options.name: High
options.description: Favors more complete reasoning at the cost of more tokens generated and slower responses

options.name: maxToolsIterations
options.description: The maximum number of tool iteration cycles the LLM will run before stopping. A single iteration can contain multiple tool calls. Set to 0 for no limit.

definitions.name: @n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.openAi
